source_pr.title: test: stable lock wait test
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

Solving #14813 
make the test more stable, calculate duration using sql statement duration

### What is changed and how it works?


### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test


Code changes



Side effects


Related changes



Release note

symptoms: PR body says "make the test more stable" for lock wait timeout by calculating duration based on the SQL execution.
failure_signature: PR body says "make the test more stable" for lock wait timeout by calculating duration based on the SQL execution.
root_cause_explanation: The lock-wait test asserted on a narrow timing window using `time.Since(start)` at assertion time, which could include extra scheduling delay and make the check flaky on CI. The PR stabilizes it by measuring the duration around the actual SQL execution (inside the goroutine) and asserting on that captured duration.
fix_pattern: Measure the lock-wait duration around the SQL execution (inside the goroutine) instead of using `time.Since(start)` at assertion time to reduce CI timing jitter.

evidence: evidence: PR body says "make the test more stable" for lock wait timeout by calculating duration based on the SQL execution.
evidence: diff measures `duration` inside the goroutine around `tk2.Exec(...)` and asserts it is within [1000ms, 1100ms], reducing timing flakiness (session/pessimistic_test.go).
